However, I can provide you with information on a plant called Chamaemelum nobile (also known as Chrysanthemum nobile), which is a different species of plant. 

Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Roman chamomile, is an annual or perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its soothing and calming properties, which have been traditionally used to relieve anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Roman chamomile include apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Roman chamomile include teas, tinctures, essential oils, and topical creams for skin and muscle relaxation.
